claim: Cited[src] and Influential[src] must be distinct types in the language womb. They are not aliases, not interchangeable, and neither subsumes the other. fn conclude(theorem, ev: Evidence[Influential, Core]) -> Proof must REJECT Evidence[Cited, Core] at typecheck with no implicit coercion. The only path from Cited to Influential is an explicit promotion receipt carrying a causal-influence trace (ablation, attention-attribution, or counterfactual probe). Symmetrically, Influential[src] must not imply Cited[src]: an answer can be causally shaped by a retrieved document the final output never names.
evidence_or_reasoning: ProvenAI (arXiv:2606.26449) separates answer correctness, citation fidelity, and document influence. A citation can be present without being the actual causal support for an answer. Source: /Users/dhyana/ds_naga_ir_language_womb_seed/naga_ir_language_womb/language/prior_art.md lines 84-91.

This boundary is currently invisible to every receipt-based audit. A prompt-injected instruction can produce output that cites a pristine source while being shaped by the injection. No existing language typechecks this distinction.

Open Questions #1 and #2 in LANGUAGE_WOMB_GRAND_CHALLENGE_SEED.md ask for the smallest type system distinguishing authority grades and whether modality is an indexed type, effect, capability, proof term, or Datalog annotation. This delta argues the evidence-modality lattice needs at least two orthogonal axes: authority (Attested_by/Tested_by/Reviewed_by/Proven_by) AND causal_role (Cited/Influential/Both/Neither).
falsification_route: Four routes: (1) Prior-art edge — show an existing language (Unison abilities, Koka effects, Rocq modules, Datalog provenance semirings, Lean typeclasses) that already types Cited and Influential as distinct and prevents silent substitution. If one exists, this delta composts into a pointer. (2) Collapse argument — show that under any reasonable evidence calculus Cited and Influential reduce to the same predicate (Belnap-Dunn or semiring provenance may already provide this collapse). (3) Cost argument — show every real LLM call fails to produce an Influential receipt, making Influential a dead type. (4) Wrong-layer argument — show this belongs in runtime spine or governance, not the type system. Counter: the seed doc explicitly asks for typechecker-level semantics.
language_impact: If accepted: (1) evidence-modality lattice gains orthogonal causal_role axis, contradicting any proposal treating citation as a single boolean predicate. (2) Typechecker rejects prompt-injection failure mode where output cites a clean source it was not shaped by. (3) Promotion from Cited to Influential requires a causal-influence receipt — a new proof-obligation kind distinct from the Attested_by -> Tested_by promotion already named in the seed.
authority_boundary: Does not authorize standing on any other claim. Does not claim ProvenAI is wrong or incomplete. Does not propose a surface syntax. Does not require operator action, external posting, or credential use. Scheduled packaging grants no standing; standing requires surviving challenge from a non-adjacent witness.
